(Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈkoːnɪŋkləkə ˈbeːrsxɔt ˈɑntʋɛrpə(ŋ) ˈklʏp]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch")), simply known as **Beerschot AC**, was a Belgian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in southern [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"). Beerschot played in the [Belgian Pro League](/wiki/Belgian_Pro_League "Belgian Pro League") from [1999–2000](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_1999%E2%80%932000 "Belgian First Division 1999–2000") (as Germinal Beerschot) until [2012–13](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Belgian_Pro_League "2012–13 Belgian Pro League"), when they were relegated not only through their league position, but also lost their professional licence through financial issues, being officially declared bankrupt on 21 May 2013, one week after the season had ended.

was founded in the town of [Ekeren](/wiki/Ekeren "Ekeren"), a northern suburb of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"). Several years earlier, in 1899, **Beerschot** was founded at Het Kiel, a southern outskirt of Antwerp, where the [1920 Olympics](/wiki/1920_Olympics "1920 Olympics") had been held. In 1971 Germinal Ekeren added the prefix *[Koninklijk](/wiki/Koninklijk "Koninklijk")* to their name (meaning *Royal* in [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")). The team reached the top division in Belgian football in 1989 and finished 13th. They achieved their highest league position of third in 1996 and 1998 and subsequently qualified for the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup "UEFA Cup"). However, in 1999, due to the low attendance of supporters and limited expansion possibilities in Ekeren, the club merged with Beerschot who were then playing in the 3rd division and themselves had severe financial problems.

kept the matricule n°3530 of Ekeren to keep their place in the first division, but retained the stadium of Beerschot, rebuilding it in the process. By keeping the matricule of Ekeren, the honours of Beerschot were considered to be distinct and separate from the new team and the club finally dropped the name Antwerpen in 2003. In 2004 further financial difficulties were experienced and [Marc Brys](/wiki/Marc_Brys "Marc Brys") was brought in as coach. He led the team to victory in the [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Cup "Belgian Cup") that year, and in doing so secured a place in [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup "UEFA Cup") for his side. After only 7 matches in the [Jupiler League 2005-06](/wiki/Jupiler_League_2005-06 "Jupiler League 2005-06") season, he was fired due to bad results (4 points from 21 and a 3–0 defeat against the 18th placed team).
+ *Runners-up:* [1997](/wiki/1997_Belgian_Super_Cup "1997 Belgian Super Cup"), [2005](/wiki/2005_Belgian_Super_Cup "2005 Belgian Super Cup")

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [K.F.C. Germinal Beerschot](/w/index.php?title=K.F.C._Germinal_Beerschot&redirect=no "K.F.C. Germinal Beerschot"))

Belgian football club

Not to be confused with [Beerschot AC (women)](/wiki/Beerschot_AC_(women) "Beerschot AC (women)"), [K. Beerschot V.A.C.](/wiki/K._Beerschot_V.A.C. "K. Beerschot V.A.C."), or [K Beerschot VA](/wiki/K_Beerschot_VA "K Beerschot VA").

Football club

Beerschot AC

**Koninklijke Beerschot Antwerpen Club** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈkoːnɪŋkləkə ˈbeːrsxɔt ˈɑntʋɛrpə(ŋ) ˈklʏp]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch")), simply known as **Beerschot AC**, was a Belgian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in southern [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"). Beerschot played in the [Belgian Pro League](/wiki/Belgian_Pro_League "Belgian Pro League") from [1999–2000](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_1999%E2%80%932000 "Belgian First Division 1999–2000") (as Germinal Beerschot) until [2012–13](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Belgian_Pro_League "2012–13 Belgian Pro League"), when they were relegated not only through their league position, but also lost their professional licence through financial issues, being officially declared bankrupt on 21 May 2013, one week after the season had ended.

**K.F.C. Germinal Ekeren**, established in 1920, rebranded themselves as Germinal Beerschot in 1999, retaining the [matricule number](/wiki/Matricule "Matricule") and history but adopting some of the identity of [K Beerschot VAC](/wiki/K._Beerschot_V.A.C. "K. Beerschot V.A.C."), seven-times Belgian champions but struggling with financial problems in the [third division](/wiki/Belgian_Third_Division "Belgian Third Division"). Germinal Ekeren had been a first division club for the past decade, and were [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Cup "Belgian Cup") winners in 1997.

Following the merger in 1999, the club moved from the Veltwijckstadion in the municipality of [Ekeren](/wiki/Ekeren "Ekeren") to the [Olympisch Stadion](/wiki/Olympisch_Stadion_(Antwerp) "Olympisch Stadion (Antwerp)") in the Kiel neighbourhood in Antwerp. Their outfits mixed the yellow and red of Germinal Ekeren with the purple of Beerschot. Their biggest rival was [Royal Antwerp F.C.](/wiki/Royal_Antwerp_F.C. "Royal Antwerp F.C.") They won the Belgian Cup in 2005.

On 17 May 2011, the club changed its name again to Koninklijke Beerschot Antwerpse Club or **Beerschot AC**. The name change was the result of an internal struggle which split the board of directors which ended with the former Germinal Ekeren board members vacating their position, giving a free path to remove the mention of Germinal[[1]](#cite_note-1) in the team's name by the new directors as part of a business plan to restore the former [K. Beerschot V.A.C.](/wiki/K._Beerschot_V.A.C. "K. Beerschot V.A.C.") to its former glory. In addition, the club set its motto to the Latin phrase 'Tene Quod Bene', which translates as 'keep what is good', again referring to the fact that only the "Beerschot" part was kept. After being relegated in [2012–13](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Belgian_Pro_League "2012–13 Belgian Pro League"), the club went bankrupt at the end of the season, was removed from competition altogether and folded shortly afterwards. In June 2013, [K.F.C.O. Wilrijk](/wiki/FCO_Beerschot_Wilrijk#KFCO_Wilrijk "FCO Beerschot Wilrijk") unofficially integrated Beerschot AC's identity into theirs to become [FCO Beerschot Wilrijk](/wiki/FCO_Beerschot_Wilrijk "FCO Beerschot Wilrijk"), moving to Beerschot AC's vacated stadium.[[2]](#cite_note-mergeBWBF-2) This new club started in the first division of the [Belgian Provincial leagues](/wiki/Belgian_Provincial_leagues "Belgian Provincial leagues").[[2]](#cite_note-mergeBWBF-2)

The youth academy of Beerschot produced the likes of [Thomas Vermaelen](/wiki/Thomas_Vermaelen "Thomas Vermaelen"), [Mousa Dembélé](/wiki/Mousa_Demb%C3%A9l%C3%A9_(Belgian_footballer) "Mousa Dembélé (Belgian footballer)"), [Radja Nainggolan](/wiki/Radja_Nainggolan "Radja Nainggolan"), [Jan Vertonghen](/wiki/Jan_Vertonghen "Jan Vertonghen"), and [Toby Alderweireld](/wiki/Toby_Alderweireld "Toby Alderweireld").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Beerschot_A.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 1920 **F.C. Germinal Ekeren** was founded in the town of [Ekeren](/wiki/Ekeren "Ekeren"), a northern suburb of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp"). Several years earlier, in 1899, **Beerschot** was founded at Het Kiel, a southern outskirt of Antwerp, where the [1920 Olympics](/wiki/1920_Olympics "1920 Olympics") had been held. In 1971 Germinal Ekeren added the prefix *[Koninklijk](/wiki/Koninklijk "Koninklijk")* to their name (meaning *Royal* in [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")). The team reached the top division in Belgian football in 1989 and finished 13th. They achieved their highest league position of third in 1996 and 1998 and subsequently qualified for the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup "UEFA Cup"). However, in 1999, due to the low attendance of supporters and limited expansion possibilities in Ekeren, the club merged with Beerschot who were then playing in the 3rd division and themselves had severe financial problems.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/f/fc/Germinal_Beerschot_Antwerpen_logo.svg/250px-Germinal_Beerschot_Antwerpen_logo.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Germinal_Beerschot_Antwerpen_logo.svg)

With the change, a new logo was also introduced, replacing the former logo of Germinal Beerschot

The new team, **K.F.C. Germinal Beerschot Antwerpen** kept the matricule n°3530 of Ekeren to keep their place in the first division, but retained the stadium of Beerschot, rebuilding it in the process. By keeping the matricule of Ekeren, the honours of Beerschot were considered to be distinct and separate from the new team and the club finally dropped the name Antwerpen in 2003. In 2004 further financial difficulties were experienced and [Marc Brys](/wiki/Marc_Brys "Marc Brys") was brought in as coach. He led the team to victory in the [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Cup "Belgian Cup") that year, and in doing so secured a place in [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup "UEFA Cup") for his side. After only 7 matches in the [Jupiler League 2005-06](/wiki/Jupiler_League_2005-06 "Jupiler League 2005-06") season, he was fired due to bad results (4 points from 21 and a 3–0 defeat against the 18th placed team).

May 2013, [Naamloze vennootschap](/wiki/Naamloze_vennootschap "Naamloze vennootschap") Beerschot announced it would be [liquidated](/wiki/Liquidated "Liquidated"). After having failed to present a financial plan to the [Royal Belgian Football Association](/wiki/Royal_Belgian_Football_Association "Royal Belgian Football Association"), the club had lost its license to play in the 1st division. Unable to attract enough financial means to continue playing in a lower division they decided upon liquidation. Initially it was unclear what would happen to the matricule or players,[[3]](#cite_note-3) but it became clear on 21 May 2013 that the club dissolved, meaning the matricule was lost and the players free to look for a new club.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a0/Beerschot_V.A.C._Performance_Chart.png/330px-Beerschot_V.A.C._Performance_Chart.png)](/wiki/File:Beerschot_V.A.C._Performance_Chart.png)

Historical league performance chart of Germinal Ekeren and Beerschot

Late May 2013 the "entourage" and fans of Beerschot started negotiations with [K.F.C.O. Wilrijk](/wiki/FCO_Beerschot_Wilrijk#KFCO_Wilrijk "FCO Beerschot Wilrijk") and [Sportkring Sint-Niklaas](/wiki/Sportkring_Sint-Niklaas "Sportkring Sint-Niklaas") in the hope of a "merge" of Beerschot with one of them.[[2]](#cite_note-mergeBWBF-2) In June 2013 when K.F.C.O. Wilrijk adapted its name into [F.C.O. Beerschot-Wilrijk](/wiki/FCO_Beerschot_Wilrijk "FCO Beerschot Wilrijk"), this "merge" took place.[[2]](#cite_note-mergeBWBF-2) The new club started in the first division of the [Belgian Provincial leagues](/wiki/Belgian_Provincial_leagues "Belgian Provincial leagues").[[2]](#cite_note-mergeBWBF-2)

### European competitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Beerschot_A.C.&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: European competitions")]

The matricule number 3530 played their first European game as KFC Germinal Ekeren in the [1991–92 season](/wiki/1991%E2%80%9392_UEFA_Cup "1991–92 UEFA Cup") against [Celtic](/wiki/Celtic_F.C. "Celtic F.C.") of Scotland (losing 2–0 away and drawing 1–1 at home). They qualified for the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup "UEFA Cup") in that year following a 5th-place finish in the championship. They then had to wait until [1995](/wiki/1995_UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "1995 UEFA Intertoto Cup") and a 6th-place finish to qualify for their next European competition ([Intertoto Cup](/wiki/Intertoto_Cup "Intertoto Cup")), finishing 2nd in Group 3 of the first round behind [FC Aarau](/wiki/FC_Aarau "FC Aarau") of Switzerland, which was insufficient to qualify for the second round. In [1996–97](/wiki/1996%E2%80%9397_UEFA_Cup "1996–97 UEFA Cup") they qualified for the UEFA Cup after finishing 3rd in the league. The club was defeated by [Grazer AK](/wiki/Grazer_AK "Grazer AK") in the first round.

In [1997–98](/wiki/1997%E2%80%9398_UEFA_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "1997–98 UEFA Cup Winners' Cup") they played the [UEFA Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "UEFA Cup Winners' Cup") after a [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Cup "Belgian Cup") win. They lost in the second round at the hands of [VfB Stuttgart](/wiki/VfB_Stuttgart "VfB Stuttgart") after a win against [FK Crvena Zvezda](/wiki/Red_Star_Belgrade "Red Star Belgrade"). The [next season](/wiki/1998%E2%80%9399_UEFA_Cup "1998–99 UEFA Cup"), they reached the UEFA Cup first preliminary round after a 3rd place, but lost in the second preliminary round against Swiss team [Servette FC](/wiki/Servette_FC "Servette FC"). In the [2008 UEFA Intertoto Cup](/wiki/2008_UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "2008 UEFA Intertoto Cup") (their only entry as Germinal Beerschot), they met [Neftchi Baku](/wiki/Neftchi_Baku_PFK "Neftchi Baku PFK") of Azerbaijan in the second round, drawing 1–1 at home but losing 0–1 away to be eliminated from the competition.

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Beerschot_A.C.&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Honours")]

* **[Belgian Second Division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Division "Belgian Second Division")**
